extraction.one_line_summary: CARAPAZ outsprinted HIGUITA after 28-rider break split into leading duo, with HIGUITA taking race lead despite losing stage
tactical_patterns: Ineos planted Carapaz in 28-rider break while UAE protected leader, forcing UAE to chase alone revealing isolation.
Higuita-Carapaz détente broke down when time gap meant GC jersey worth more than stage cooperation arrangement.
Six-rider chase including Quintana failed because Ayuso rode without radio, causing timing miscommunication and regrouping delays.
Ineos deployed Carapaz in break despite having no GC position, pure stage-hunting freed him from pace-setting duties.

rider_signals.HIGUITA Sergio: positive: Colombian champion initiated early Cat-1 break split and nearly held off Carapaz sprint despite carrying GC defense burden
rider_signals.CARAPAZ Richard: positive: Timed final 1300m attack perfectly, closed bike-length deficit in final 100m against motivated GC rival
rider_signals.AYUSO Juan: negative: Rode bridging effort without race radio creating coordination failures, showed individual strength but tactical naivety
rider_signals.ALMEIDA João: negative: Lost race lead by allowing 28-rider break with two strong climbers, UAE isolated doing all chase work
rider_signals.QUINTANA Nauru: negative: Joined six-rider chase group but couldn't prevent losing race he previously led, showing declining form
rider_signals.GROVES Kaden: neutral: DSM gambled on Van der Poel leadout, Groves took third from reduced bunch sprint
